sections.paragraph:
ὄρνεον, ου, τό (a dim. form=ὄρνις; Hom. et al.; IG IV2/1, 93, 17 [III/IV a.d.]; PPetr III, 71 [III b.c.]; PRyl 98[a], 9; PLond III, 1259, 16 p. 240 [IV a.d.]; LXX; TestSol; JosAs 11:1 cod. A; GrBar, Philo; Jos., Ant. 1, 184; 3, 25; 18, 195) bird Rv 19:17, 21. πᾶν ὄρ. ἀκάθαρτον καὶ μεμισημένον every bird that is unclean and detestable (for cultic reasons, e.g. the owl, heron, pelican, great horned owl) 18:2 (cp. Dt 14:11 πᾶν ὄρνεον καθαρόν). Of the phoenix 1 Cl 25:2; 26:1. Pl., of various unclean birds (cp. Dt 14:11ff) 10:4. W. κτῆνος Hs 9, 1, 8.—DELG s.v. ὄρνις. M-M.

dim. dim. = diminutive

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

PPetr PPetr = The Flinders Petrie Papyri—List 4

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 724
